Download ash ISO v2025.06.01

2.3 GB • SHA256 + minisign + cosign + SLSA verified • Boots in ~45s • Arch + GNOME + AI tools

2.3 GB (2,469,396,480 bytes) SHA256: a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2… v2025.06.01 • 2025-01-15
Download ISO (2.3 GB)
Verify Before Boot (Required)

1. SHA256

sha256sum ash-2025.06.01.iso
# Expected: a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2

2. minisign (Recommended)

minisign -Vm ash-2025.06.01.iso -P RWQf6LRCGA9i52mlZT2k5B5Y5Q5Y5Q5Y5Q5Y5Q5Y5Q5Y5Q5Y5Q5Y5Q5Y= -x ash-2025.06.01.iso.minisig

3. cosign (Keyless + Transparency Log)

cosign verify-blob --bundle ash-2025.06.01.iso.cosign.bundle \
  --certificate-identity-regexp "https://github.com/ash-linux/ash/.github/workflows/release.yml@refs/tags/v*" \
  --certificate-oidc-issuer "https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com" \
  ash-2025.06.01.iso

VMware Fusion / Workstation

  1. File → New → "Install from disc or image"
  2. Select ash-2025.06.01.iso
  3. Guest OS: Linux → Arch Linux 64-bit
  4. Enable GPU: Settings → Display → "Accelerate 3D graphics" + "Use host GPU"
  5. 8GB+ RAM, 4+ CPU cores, 50GB+ disk
  6. Boot → GRUB → Select "ash" → Desktop in ~45s

Fusion 13+ / Workstation 17+ required for best GPU support.

VirtualBox 7+

  1. New → Name: "ash" → Type: Linux → Version: Arch Linux (64-bit)
  2. 8GB+ RAM, 4+ CPUs
  3. Create new disk: 50GB+ (VDI, dynamically allocated)
  4. Settings → System → Processor → Enable PAE/NX
  5. Settings → Display → Video Memory: 128MB → Enable 3D Acceleration
  6. Settings → Storage → Optical Drive → Choose ISO
  7. Boot → Desktop in ~65s

GPU passthrough: VBoxManage modifyvm "ash" --gpupassthrough on (host GPU required).

Parallels Desktop 18+

  1. File → New → "Install Windows or another OS from a DVD or image file"
  2. Select ISO → Continue → "Linux" → "Arch Linux"
  3. Customize before install: 8GB+ RAM, 4+ CPUs
  4. Hardware → Graphics → "Auto" (Metal acceleration)
  5. Boot → Desktop in ~45s (Apple Silicon) / ~55s (Intel)

Apple Silicon: Metal GPU acceleration works automatically. Intel Mac: enable "Passthrough" in GPU settings.

QEMU/KVM / libvirt

# Quick boot (no install)
qemu-system-x86_64 \
  -enable-kvm -cpu host -m 4G -smp 4 \
  -drive file=ash-2025.06.01.iso,media=cdrom,readonly=on \
  -boot d -display gtk -serial stdio \
  -device virtio-net-pci,netdev=net0 \
  -netdev user,id=net0,hostfwd=tcp::2222-:22 \
  -device virtio-gpu-pci

# Install to disk (persistent)
qemu-img create -f qcow2 ash-disk.qcow2 50G
qemu-system-x86_64 \
  -enable-kvm -cpu host -m 8G -smp 4 \
  -drive file=ash-2025.06.01.iso,media=cdrom,readonly=on \
  -drive file=ash-disk.qcow2,format=qcow2 \
  -boot d -display gtk \
  -device virtio-net-pci,netdev=net0 \
  -netdev user,id=net0,hostfwd=tcp::2222-:22 \
  -device virtio-gpu-pci

For GPU passthrough: add -device vfio-pci,host=XX:XX.X with IOMMU configured.
